AddServerInput.title: AddServerInput
AddServerInput.description: Input model for adding a new MCP server.

path.title: Path
path.description: Slug-relative path, including the top-level directory (e.g. 'render/app.jsx', 'queries/foo.sql.j2', 'analysis/intent.md').

ArtifactFile.title: ArtifactFile
ArtifactFile.description:
Flat-wire entry for a single artifact file inside ``dashboards/<slug>/``
(and, by symmetry, ``reports/<slug>/`` on the SaaS side).

``path`` is slug-relative, including the top-level directory
(e.g. ``render/charts/trend.jsx``, ``queries/sales.sql.j2``,
``analysis/insights.json``). Matches ``IArtifactFileEntry`` on TS.

slug.title: Slug
slug.description: Filesystem-friendly slug; doubles as the artifact's directory name.

description.title: Description
description.description: One-paragraph description of what this artifact does.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp at second precision.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp refreshed on every save_query / save_query_template / bind_existing_* call so list pages can order by recency. ``None`` until the first mutation after creation. Older manifests written before this field was introduced deserialize cleanly with ``None``.

datasources.title: Datasources
datasources.description: Distinct datasource labels referenced by this artifact's queries, populated incrementally as ``save_query`` / ``save_query_template`` is called. Used by the subagent that gets spawned for follow-up questions to know which connectors to bind. Stable order = first-seen order so diffs stay readable.

key_tables.title: Key Tables
key_tables.description: Distinct table references across this artifact's queries, preserving the qualification each SQL used (``finbench.main.Account`` stays as is; bare ``Account`` stays bare). Code-generated at finalize time by parsing every ``queries/*.sql`` and ``queries/*.sql.j2`` with sqlglot, stripping CTE aliases, and collapsing same-table-different-qualification entries to the more informative form — the LLM never writes this field. The follow-up ask agent reads it both to skip ``list_tables`` / ``describe_table`` round-trips AND to copy the exact qualified reference when planning new SQL on related tables (so the query runs on strict-schema dialects without guessing the prefix). Empty when finalize hasn't run yet or when SQL parsing failed across the board (rare). Sorted alphabetically for diff stability.

ArtifactManifest.title: ArtifactManifest
ArtifactManifest.description:
Persisted at ``<root>/<slug>/manifest.json``.

Field choices:

* ``slug`` is the LLM-supplied stable identifier; it doubles as the
  on-disk directory name (``reports/<slug>/`` /
  ``dashboards/<slug>/``). The LLM is responsible for choosing a
  slug that doesn't collide with any existing artifact directory
  (system prompt mandates a ``glob`` of the kind root before
  calling ``start_new_*``).
* ``name`` and ``description`` are **required, non-empty**. The
  system prompt forces the LLM to produce both at ``start_new_*``
  time so the artifact is never orphaned without a display name.
* ``kind`` mirrors the parent directory (``"reports"`` →
  ``"report"``, ``"dashboards"`` → ``"dashboard"``); callers that
  read the file by path already know which kind it is, but keeping
  the field self-describing means a single backend route can serve
  both shapes by inspecting one file.
* ``created_at`` is the UTC timestamp at which the manifest was
  first written.
* ``updated_at`` is a persisted ISO-8601 UTC timestamp refreshed on
  every ``save_query`` / ``save_query_template`` / ``bind_existing_*``
  call so list pages can order by recency. ``None`` until the first
  mutation after creation; older manifests written before this field
  was introduced deserialize cleanly with ``None``.

build_mode.title: Build Mode
build_mode.description: 'check' returns existing store stats; 'overwrite' clears and rebuilds.

pool_size.title: Pool Size
pool_size.description: Thread pool size for parallel processing.

source.title: Source
source.description: GitHub repo 'owner/repo', URL, or local path.

github_ref.title: Github Ref
github_ref.description: Git branch / tag / commit for GitHub sources.

github_token.title: Github Token
github_token.description: GitHub API token for authenticated access.

max_depth.title: Max Depth
max_depth.description: Max crawl depth for website sources.

BootstrapDocInput.title: BootstrapDocInput
BootstrapDocInput.description:
POST body for /api/v1/kb/bootstrap-docs.

Only ``platform`` is required.  Every other field falls back to the
matching ``DocumentConfig`` in ``agent.yml`` (``agent.document.<platform>``).

components.title: Components
components.description: Knowledge base components to initialize. `metadata` scans live database schema and sample rows; `semantic_model` derives semantic schema objects from success-story SQLs; `metrics` derives MetricFlow-style business metrics; `reference_sql` indexes reusable SQL files.

strategy.title: Strategy
strategy.description: Update strategy. `check` inspects existing data without rebuilding where supported, `overwrite` clears and rebuilds, and `incremental` appends or updates changed entries.

schema_linking_type.title: Schema Linking Type
schema_linking_type.description: Metadata-only option controlling which object types are indexed from the database. Expected values follow `datus-agent bootstrap-kb`: `table`, `view`, `mv`, or `full`.

catalog.title: Catalog
catalog.description: Optional metadata catalog filter. Mainly relevant for engines with a catalog layer, such as Snowflake or StarRocks.

database_name.title: Database Name
database_name.description: Optional metadata database filter. Passed through to adapter-specific initialization; for Snowflake it maps to schema name, while for MySQL/PostgreSQL/StarRocks it is the database name.

success_story.title: Success Story
success_story.description: Project-root-relative path to a success-story CSV containing historical question/SQL pairs. Used by `semantic_model` and `metrics` when bootstrapping from success stories.

subject_tree.title: Subject Tree
subject_tree.description: Optional predefined hierarchical categories in `domain/layer1/layer2` form, such as `Sales/Reporting/Daily`. Used by `metrics` and `reference_sql`; if omitted, bootstrap reuses or learns categories from existing KB content.

sql_dir.title: Sql Dir
sql_dir.description: Project-root-relative directory containing `.sql` files for the `reference_sql` component. Files are scanned recursively and only `SELECT` statements are indexed.

BootstrapKbInput.title: BootstrapKbInput
BootstrapKbInput.description:
POST body for /api/v1/kb/bootstrap.

Mirrors the subset of `datus-agent bootstrap-kb` options exposed by the backend API.

CallToolInput.title: CallToolInput
CallToolInput.description: Input model for calling a tool on an MCP server.

ChannelBinding.title: ChannelBinding
ChannelBinding.description:
The channel state for one sub-agent.

Carries the full desired channel list for the agent being edited; the
backend replaces only that agent's entries in the global ``channels`` map
(matched by ``subagent_id``). An empty list clears this agent's channels.

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: Whether this channel's gateway adapter is active.

name.title: Name
name.description: Reference name, used as the channel config key.

secrets.title: Secrets
secrets.description: Adapter-specific secret fields (e.g. app_token/bot_token). Written into `extra` verbatim.

ChannelInput.title: ChannelInput
ChannelInput.description:
One IM gateway channel binding submitted with an agent edit.

Each channel carries its own ``enabled`` switch (adapters toggle
independently) and a generic ``secrets`` bag whose keys depend on the
adapter ``type`` — e.g. ``app_token`` / ``bot_token`` for slack. Values are
written verbatim into the ``agent.yml`` ``channels`` section (no
encryption); ``${ENV_VAR}`` placeholders are resolved at load time.

columns.title: Columns
columns.description: All column names in the dataset

x_col.title: X Col
x_col.description: X-axis column (absent when chart_type is Unknown)

y_cols.title: Y Cols
y_cols.description: Y-axis column(s) (absent when chart_type is Unknown)

user_query.title: User Query
user_query.description: First user message in the session

datasource_id.title: Datasource Id
datasource_id.description: Datasource ID this agent is bound to

type.title: Type
type.description: Node class: gen_sql / gen_report / ask_report / ask_dashboard / ...

artifact_slug.title: Artifact Slug
artifact_slug.description: Slug of the visual report / dashboard this agent is bound to. Required when ``type`` is ``ask_report`` or ``ask_dashboard``; ignored for other types. Must match ``^[a-z0-9_]{1,80}$``. The matching ``reports/<slug>`` or ``dashboards/<slug>`` directory must already exist.

CreateAgentInput.title: CreateAgentInput
CreateAgentInput.description: Input for creating a new sub-agent.

subject_path.title: Subject Path
subject_path.description: Parent path where directory will be created

role_type.title: Role Type
role_type.description: 角色类型: resource 或 datasource

CsvData.title: CsvData
CsvData.description: Tabular data sent by the frontend.

description.title: Description
description.description: One-paragraph description of what the dashboard tracks (manifest.json)

manifest.description: Full manifest.json contents (slug + name + description + kind + created_at)

files.title: Files
files.description: Flat list of every artifact file under dashboards/<slug>/ that passes the per-prefix allowlist (render/{.jsx,.js,.css}, queries/{.sql.j2,.params.json}, analysis/{.md,.json}). manifest.json is intentionally NOT included — the parsed structured form is on ``manifest`` above. Sorted by path.

templates.title: Templates
templates.description: Per-slug Jinja2 template metadata (params declaration, columns, sample_params)

DashboardDetail.title: DashboardDetail
DashboardDetail.description:
Wire shape of ``GET /api/v1/dashboard/detail`` (agent-only path).

``files`` is the slug-relative flat list covering every artifact file
the dashboard owns (render/ tree + queries/*.sql.j2 / queries/*.params.json
+ analysis/). ``templates`` stays as a parsed sidecar for outer-panel
UI that wants to drive filter affordances without re-parsing the
.params.json bytes; the iframe itself only needs the render slice of
``files``.

A downstream SaaS host extends this with the publication-side
fields (``subagent`` / ``dashboard_id`` / ``published_version`` /
``published_at``) via its own subclass when a Postgres deployment
is present.

query_slug.title: Query Slug
query_slug.description: Query template slug — the filename stem of the ``dashboards/<dashboard_slug>/queries/<query_slug>.sql.j2`` + ``.params.json`` sibling pair.

params.title: Params
params.description: User-selected filter values keyed by param name

published_version.title: Published Version
published_version.description: When set, render the template from an immutable snapshot at this version instead of the on-disk buffer. Only supported by a SaaS host with a version-snapshot store; the agent-only ``datus --web`` path rejects non-null values with INVALID_PUBLISHED_VERSION.

DataInsight.title: DataInsight
DataInsight.description: Context metadata extracted from SQL and data analysis.

data_insight.description: Context metadata (present when sql is provided)

chart_type.title: Chart Type
chart_type.description: Desired chart type; omit for auto-recommendation

sql.title: Sql
sql.description: SQL query that produced the data (enables metadata extraction)

current.title: Current
current.description: Whether this is the current database

tables_count.title: Tables Count
tables_count.description: Number of tables in the database

subject_path.title: Subject Path
subject_path.description: Path of the subject to delete

artifact_slug.title: Artifact Slug
artifact_slug.description: Echoed by the SaaS UI when re-saving an ask_report / ask_dashboard agent. The backend treats this field as IMMUTABLE — if it differs from the persisted binding the request is rejected with ``IMMUTABLE_FIELD``. Identical / omitted values are silently dropped.

channels.description: IM gateway channel bindings for this agent. Omit to leave channels untouched.

EditAgentInput.title: EditAgentInput
EditAgentInput.description: Input for editing an existing sub-agent. Only provided fields are updated.

EditSemanticModelInput.title: EditSemanticModelInput
EditSemanticModelInput.description: Edit semantic model entry (table or column).

execute_task_id.title: Execute Task Id
execute_task_id.description: Task ID for this SQL execution, can be used to stop it

system.title: System
system.description: Whether this is a system command

execute_task_id.title: Execute Task Id
execute_task_id.description: Caller-supplied task ID used to identify this execution so it can later be cancelled via /sql/stop_execute. Returned unchanged in ExecuteSQLData. If omitted, the server generates one.

message.title: Message
message.description: Leave empty; server derives it from reaction fields

model.title: Model
model.description: Per-request model override in 'provider/model_id' format (e.g. 'openai/gpt-4.1', 'custom/my-model'). Takes highest priority over all other model config.

interactive.title: Interactive
interactive.description: Override server default for ask_user interactive tool. None = use server default.

workspace_root.title: Workspace Root
workspace_root.description: Root directory path for filesystem MCP server

stream_response.title: Stream Response
stream_response.description: Whether to stream response; None means use server default

source_session_id.title: Source Session Id
source_session_id.description: Session that produced the message being reacted to

reference_msg.title: Reference Msg
reference_msg.description: Text of the bot message the user reacted to

reaction_msg.title: Reaction Msg
reaction_msg.description: Optional free-text comment attached to the reaction

FeedbackChatInput.title: FeedbackChatInput
FeedbackChatInput.description:
Input for /chat/feedback — reaction-triggered feedback agent.

``message`` is server-rendered from the reaction fields via
:func:`datus.utils.feedback_prompt.build_reaction_feedback_prompt`, so callers
can leave it empty.

example.message: Show me total sales for last month

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: Task ID that feedback was recorded for

recorded_at.title: Recorded At
recorded_at.description: ISO timestamp when feedback was recorded

success.title: Success
success.description: Whether the execution is successful or not, 1 is success, 0 is failure

error.title: Error
error.description: Error message: field is not empty when success=0

IMessageContent.title: IMessageContent
IMessageContent.description: Message content with type and payload.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Session id the message was queued for

queued_count.title: Queued Count
queued_count.description: Number of pending items in the queue after this push

message.title: Message
message.description: Free-text user message to inject into the agent's pending input queue

InsertMessageInput.title: InsertMessageInput
InsertMessageInput.description: Input for appending a free-text user message to a running chat.

KbComponent.title: KbComponent
KbComponent.description: Knowledge base components that can be bootstrapped.

is_primary_key.title: Is Primary Key
is_primary_key.description: Whether the dimension is a primary key

MetricDimensionItem.title: MetricDimensionItem
MetricDimensionItem.description: A queryable dimension of a metric.

invalid_dimensions.title: Invalid Dimensions
invalid_dimensions.description: Requested dimensions the metric does not support

MetricDimensionPreflight.title: MetricDimensionPreflight
MetricDimensionPreflight.description: Why the requested dimensions are not all supported by the metric.

MetricDimensionsData.title: MetricDimensionsData
MetricDimensionsData.description: Available dimensions for a saved metric.

sql.title: Sql
sql.description: Runnable SQL compiled from the metric definition

database.title: Database
database.description: Physical database the SQL should run against

preflight_error.description: Set instead of sql when the requested dimensions are invalid

MetricPreviewData.title: MetricPreviewData
MetricPreviewData.description: Compiled SQL for previewing a metric's data, or a dimension preflight error.

subject_path.title: Subject Path
subject_path.description: Path to the saved metric; the leaf is the metric name

time_start.title: Time Start
time_start.description: Optional start time (ISO or relative, e.g. '-7d')

time_end.title: Time End
time_end.description: Optional end time (ISO or relative, e.g. 'now')

where.title: Where
where.description: Optional SQL WHERE clause (without the WHERE keyword)

order_by.title: Order By
order_by.description: Optional order-by columns; prefix '-' for descending

MetricPreviewInput.title: MetricPreviewInput
MetricPreviewInput.description: Preview a saved metric by compiling it to SQL (dry-run).

provider.title: Provider
provider.description: Provider key from providers.yml, or 'custom' for agent.models entries

id.title: Id
id.description: Model slug as consumed by the SDK

model.title: Model
model.description: Actual model name (same as id for provider models, ModelConfig.model for custom)

ModelInfo.title: ModelInfo
ModelInfo.description: A single model entry returned by the catalog endpoint.

ModelPricing.title: ModelPricing
ModelPricing.description:
Per-token pricing for a single model, in OpenRouter's native format.

Values are preserved as strings to avoid floating-point rounding at the API
boundary. Units are USD per token unless the upstream source overrides.

providers.title: Providers
providers.description: Provider keys represented in this response

fetched_at.title: Fetched At
fetched_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the OpenRouter cache

source.title: Source
source.description: Where the data came from: cache or catalog

ProbeDatasourceRequest.title: ProbeDatasourceRequest
ProbeDatasourceRequest.description: Single datasource config dict — flat shape matching IDatasourceConfig.

ProbeModelRequest.title: ProbeModelRequest
ProbeModelRequest.description: Single LLM model config dict — flat shape matching IModelInfo.

params.title: Params
params.description: Parameters declared by the SQL template

sample_params.title: Sample Params
sample_params.description: Values used for the trial render

QueryTemplateMetaFile.title: QueryTemplateMetaFile
QueryTemplateMetaFile.description:
Schema for ``queries/<slug>.params.json`` files.

Companion to the ``.sql.j2`` template body — declares the params the
template references plus the column metadata inferred from the trial
render the LLM did with ``sample_params``.

subject_path.title: Subject Path
subject_path.description: Parent path where reference SQL will be created

description.title: Description
description.description: One-paragraph description of what the report covers (manifest.json)

manifest.description: Full manifest.json contents (slug + name + description + kind + created_at)

files.title: Files
files.description: Flat list of every artifact file under reports/<slug>/ that passes the per-prefix allowlist (render/{.jsx,.js,.css,.json,.md}, queries/{.sql,.json}, analysis/{.md,.json}). manifest.json is intentionally NOT included — the parsed structured form is on ``manifest`` above. Sorted by path.

ReportDetail.title: ReportDetail
ReportDetail.description:
Wire shape of ``GET /api/v1/report/detail`` (agent-only path).

``files`` is the slug-relative flat list covering every artifact file
the report owns (render/ tree + queries/<slug>.sql / .json pre-baked
result pairs + analysis/ sidecars). Unlike dashboards, reports inline
their query results into the bundle — there's no live-query path at
view time, so ``@datus/web-common/modules/report`` (and the standalone
``@datus/web-artifact-render`` UMD viewer it ships with) only needs
this list to render the entire artifact.

A downstream SaaS host extends this with the publication-side
fields (``subagent`` / ``report_id`` / ``published_version`` /
``published_at``) via its own subclass when a Postgres deployment
is present.

from_event_id.title: From Event Id
from_event_id.description: Event cursor to resume from; omit to auto-resume

ResumeChatInput.title: ResumeChatInput
ResumeChatInput.description: Input for reconnecting to a running chat task.

current_date.title: Current Date
current_date.description: Current date reference for relative time expressions

ext_knowledge.title: Ext Knowledge
ext_knowledge.description: Supplementary description / evidence supplied with the question

parent_action_id.title: Parent Action Id
parent_action_id.description: Parent action ID for sub-agent grouping

type.title: Type
type.description: 权限类型: feature 或 datasource

SetPermissionsRequest.title: SetPermissionsRequest
SetPermissionsRequest.description: 批量设置权限请求模型。

SetRolePermissionsRequest.description: 设置角色权限请求模型。

SetUserRolesRequest.title: SetUserRolesRequest
SetUserRolesRequest.description: 设置用户角色请求模型。

executed_at.title: Executed At
executed_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the executing query

datasource.title: Datasource
datasource.description: Logical datasource the query ran against

rows.title: Rows
rows.description: Result rows; each is a {column → scalar} map

sql.title: Sql
sql.description: The fully rendered SQL the connector executed (after Jinja2 + bind-value substitution). Surfaced so the in-iframe <ChartEntryMore> menu can show 'View SQL'.

SqlQueryResultEnvelope.title: SqlQueryResultEnvelope
SqlQueryResultEnvelope.description:
Matches the ISqlQueryResult shape consumed by ``useQuerySql`` on the frontend.

The frontend hook destructures ``data?.rows ?? []`` / ``data?.columns ?? []``
and reads ``data?.sql`` for the optional "View SQL" affordance — keep the
field names in lockstep with ``packages/web-artifact/src/types.ts``.

StopChatInput.description: Input for stopping a running chat session.

execute_task_id.title: Execute Task Id
execute_task_id.description: Task ID of the stopped SQL execution

stopped.title: Stopped
stopped.description: Whether the execution was successfully stopped

StopExecuteSQLData.title: StopExecuteSQLData
StopExecuteSQLData.description: Data for stop SQL execution result.

execute_task_id.title: Execute Task Id
execute_task_id.description: Task ID of the SQL execution to stop

StopExecuteSQLInput.title: StopExecuteSQLInput
StopExecuteSQLInput.description: Input model for stopping a SQL execution.

model.title: Model
model.description: Per-request model override in 'provider/model_id' format (e.g. 'openai/gpt-4.1', 'custom/my-model'). Takes highest priority over all other model config.

interactive.title: Interactive
interactive.description: Override server default for ask_user interactive tool. None = use server default.

workspace_root.title: Workspace Root
workspace_root.description: Root directory path for filesystem MCP server

stream_response.title: Stream Response
stream_response.description: Whether to stream response; None means use server default

subagent_id.title: Subagent Id
subagent_id.description: Subagent ID (builtin name or DB SubAgent id)

language.title: Language
language.description: Response language override for all agentic node outputs (e.g. 'en', 'zh'). Falls back to agent.language from yaml when unset.

source_session_id.title: Source Session Id
source_session_id.description: Source session to seed the agent with (currently used by feedback agent to copy history).

permission_mode.title: Permission Mode
permission_mode.description: Per-request permission profile override. When set, the chat node's PermissionManager is switched to this profile for the duration of the request without mutating shared AgentConfig state — required so concurrent SaaS users don't pollute each other's profile. None falls back to ``agent_config.active_profile_name``.

example.message: Show me total sales for last month

csv_path.description: Absolute path to the CSV file that was appended

subagent_name.description: Canonical subagent directory name used for storage

session_link.description: Echoed session link, if one was provided

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: UTC timestamp of the write (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS)

session_link.title: Session Link
session_link.description: Fully qualified URL that reopens the session in the UI. Clients should pass the URL they would use to reopen the session; leave empty if not applicable.

SuccessStoryInput.title: SuccessStoryInput
SuccessStoryInput.description:
Input model for saving a success story.

The CSV row is keyed by ``subagent_id``; the server resolves the id to
a canonical, safe directory name before writing.

Full desired state for `services.datasources`.

Any existing datasource key absent from `datasources` will be deleted.

Optional full-replace for `models` and/or update to `target`.

At least one of `models` or `target` must be provided.
